title: Quality Assuarance Manager
description:
Position: Quality Assurance Manager (QAM)
Industry: Aerospace MRO Organization
Location: Singapore

Role Overview

The Quality Assurance Manager (QAM) is responsible for establishing, maintaining, and independently managing the Company’s Quality System to ensure full compliance with:

  • Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) Part 145 Airworthiness requirements
  • Federal Aviation Authority (FAA) Part 145 regulations
  • ISO 9001:2015 Quality Management Standards

The QAM maintains close liaison with CAAS, FAA, and other relevant Airworthiness Authorities on all matters affecting airworthiness and organizational approvals. The QAM has direct access to the Accountable Manager to report any discrepancies that are not being adequately addressed.

Key Responsibilities

Quality System & Compliance

  • Establish, maintain, and administer an independent Quality System compliant with CAAS Part 145 / Part M, FAA regulations, and ISO 9001:2015 standards.
  • Develop, implement, and manage the Internal Quality Audit Program to ensure compliance with all approved maintenance procedures.
  • Identify non-compliances, raise findings through the relevant Manager, and define timelines for corrective action.
  • Ensure prompt and effective corrective actions are implemented for all identified discrepancies.
  • Review, amend, and maintain the Maintenance Organization Exposition (MOE) in accordance with CAAS, FAA, and ISO standards.

Regulatory & Authority Liaison

  • Act as the primary liaison with CAAS and FAA on all matters affecting airworthiness and approvals.
  • Conduct internal and external audits, including CAAS and FAA surveillance audits.
  • Interview and nominate Approved Signatories (CRS/ARC) to CAAS for approval.
  • Grant and administer certification privileges within the organization in accordance with CAAS and FAA requirements.

Audit, Reliability & Defect Monitoring

  • Perform reliability monitoring of components and serviced items to identify adverse trends.
  • Conduct defect analysis and ensure timely corrective and preventive actions.
  • Ensure facility test equipment is maintained, calibrated, and compliant with regulatory and ISO standards.

Suppliers, Subcontractors & External Services

  • Assess subcontractors for inclusion within the Quality System in line with CAAS, FAA, and ISO requirements.
  • Evaluate external specialist services on a case-by-case basis for maintenance activities.
  • Assess suppliers and vendors of new and used components and materials for product quality and compliance with CAAS, FAA, and ISO standards.
  • Administer and maintain Approved Supplier and Subcontractor Lists.

Documentation, Records & Technical Control

  • Prepare, implement, and maintain standard practices and procedures derived from approved sources.
  • Manage the Technical Library and ensure all technical publications and airworthiness data are current and compliant.
  • Manage the Technical Records System.
  • Ensure repair records and malfunction reports are accurate, complete, and compliant with CAAS, FAA, and ISO standards.

Training & Competency

  • Ensure training of all inspection personnel in accordance with regulatory and ISO requirements.
  • Ensure all technical personnel understand current Quality Procedures, ISO standards, and Regulatory Requirements.

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years’ experience as a Quality Assurance Manager or Assistant Quality Assurance Manager in a CAAS- and FAA-regulated aviation organization.
  • Strong working knowledge of CAAS Airworthiness requirements, FAA regulations, ISO 9001:2015, Part 145/Part M, MOE, and Maintenance Organization compliance.
  • Proven experience handling internal and external regulatory audits.
  • Ability to operate independently with strong professional judgement.
  • Clear communication skills and the confidence to escalate issues appropriately.

Why Join Us

  • Lead and strengthen the organization's compliance with CAAS, FAA, and ISO 9001:2015 standards.
  • Strong emphasis on regulatory integrity, quality independence, and operational excellence.

Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume with CAAS/FAA-relevant certifications and experience.
